grants.name: Research and Development Tax Incentive (R&DTI)
grants.description: The Australian Government's flagship R&D support program helping companies innovate by offsetting eligible R&D costs. Provides a refundable tax offset of 43.5% for small companies (turnover under $20M) and a non-refundable 38.5% offset for larger companies. Covers wages, consumables, contractor costs, and depreciation on R&D assets.
grants.amount_note: Refundable 43.5% tax offset for companies with turnover < $20M. Non-refundable 38.5% for larger companies. No cap on eligible expenditure.
eligibility: Must register with AusIndustry before tax return lodgement

grants.name: Export Market Development Grant (EMDG)
grants.description: Grants program helping Australian small and medium enterprises start and grow exports in international markets. Covers export marketing and promotional activities plus export training. Three tiers: ready to export (Tier 1), exporting within existing markets (Tier 2), and new key markets (Tier 3). Also supports representative bodies.
grants.amount_note: Tier 1: up to $30K. Tier 2: up to $50K. Tier 3: up to $80K per round. Reimbursement of eligible export promotion expenses.

grants.name: Industry Growth Program
grants.agency: Department of Industry, Science and Resources
grants.description: Provides advisory services and grant funding for innovative SMEs undertaking commercialisation and growth projects aligned with the National Reconstruction Fund priority areas. Includes expert advisory support plus grants of $50K–$250K for early-stage commercialisation and $100K–$5M for larger growth projects. Covers value-add in resources, agriculture, transport, medical science, renewables, defence, and enabling capabilities.
grants.amount_note: Early-stage: $50K–$250K. Growth/commercialisation: $100K–$5M. Advisory service included at no cost to eligible businesses.

grants.name: Entrepreneurs' Programme — Accelerating Commercialisation
grants.description: Part of the Entrepreneurs' Programme providing matched grant funding to help businesses commercialise novel products, processes, and services. Supports activities from proof-of-concept through to market launch. Provides expert facilitation and commercialisation guidance alongside funding.
grants.amount_note: Matched funding up to 50% of project costs. Maximum $1M per project. Includes facilitation support.

grants.name: CSIRO Kick-Start Program
grants.agency: CSIRO (Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation)
grants.description: Dollar-matched funding paired with access to CSIRO's world-class research expertise for innovative Australian start-ups and SMEs. Supports R&D activities including prototype development, feasibility studies, and product testing. Projects run up to 12 months with CSIRO researchers.
grants.amount_note: Matched funding $10K–$50K. Dollar-for-dollar matching required. Access to CSIRO researchers included.

grants.name: Small Business Export Loan
grants.description: Government-backed export finance for Australian SMEs to support export contracts, working capital, equipment purchases, marketing, and online international growth. Loans up to $350K with flexible repayment terms. Helps businesses fulfil export orders and manage cash flow when expanding internationally.
grants.amount_note: Loans up to $350K. Flexible terms. Covers working capital, equipment, marketing, and online export growth.
eligibility: Export or export supply chain activity

grants.name: Early Stage Innovation Company (ESIC) Tax Incentives
grants.description: Tax incentives for early-stage investors in qualifying innovative startups. Provides a 20% non-refundable carry-forward tax offset (capped at $200K per investor per year) and capital gains tax exemption for shares held 1–10 years. Encourages angel and venture capital investment in Australian innovation.
grants.amount_note: 20% tax offset on investments up to $200K per investor/year. CGT exemption for qualifying shares held 1–10 years.
eligibility: Must qualify as an Early Stage Innovation Company

grants.name: Indigenous Business Australia — Business Development & Assistance
grants.description: IBA provides business loans, working capital, performance bonds, and business support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people starting, acquiring, or growing businesses. Loans from $1K to $5M+ with tailored business advice, mentoring, and workshops. Supports economic independence for Indigenous Australians.
grants.amount_note: Business loans from $1K to $5M+. Includes business support, mentoring, and workshops. Competitive interest rates.
eligibility: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander person
Starting, acquiring, or growing a business

grants.name: NSW Minimum Viable Product (MVP) Ventures Program
grants.description: NSW state government grant supporting early-stage startups and innovative SMEs to develop a minimum viable product and progress to commercialisation. Provides matched funding for product development, market validation, and commercialisation activities. Focused on tech-enabled and deep-tech sectors.
grants.amount_note: Matched funding up to $50K. For product development, MVP creation, and commercialisation activities.

grants.name: NSW Research & Development Tax Incentive Top-Up
grants.description: NSW state-level incentive providing a 15% refundable R&D tax offset for eligible small companies with annual NSW R&D expenditure of $25K to $1M. Stacks with the federal R&DTI to reduce effective R&D costs. Designed to retain and grow R&D activity in NSW.
grants.amount_note: 15% refundable offset on eligible NSW R&D expenditure. Capped at $150K per company per year. Stacks with federal R&DTI.

grants.name: NSW SafeWork Small Business Rebate
grants.description: NSW government rebate providing up to $1,000 for small businesses to purchase safety equipment or implement workplace health and safety improvements. Helps reduce workplace injuries and improve compliance with WHS regulations. Quick and simple application process.
grants.amount_note: Up to $1,000 rebate for safety equipment and WHS improvements. One rebate per ABN.

grants.name: VIC Business Support Fund — Digital & Innovation
grants.agency: Business Victoria / Department of Jobs, Skills, Industry and Regions
grants.description: Victorian state government suite of grants for business innovation, digital transformation, and capability building. Includes digital adoption grants, innovation vouchers, and capability uplift programs. Helps Victorian businesses adopt technology, improve processes, and develop new products.
grants.amount_note: Grants typically $5K–$100K depending on stream. Co-contribution of 25%–50% required. Multiple streams available.

grants.name: LaunchVic — Victorian Startup Ecosystem Support
grants.description: Victoria's dedicated startup agency providing grants and support to accelerators, incubators, founder programs, and startup ecosystem builders. Supports pre-revenue to Series A startups through funded programs. Focus on building Victoria's startup ecosystem through infrastructure and capability development.
grants.amount_note: Grants for ecosystem partners (accelerators, incubators, programs). Amounts vary widely — $50K–$500K+. Founder grants also available.
eligibility: Accelerator, incubator, or founder support program

grants.name: VIC Made in Victoria — Manufacturing Growth Program
grants.description: Victorian state grant supporting manufacturing businesses to invest in new equipment, technology adoption, process improvement, and job creation. Part of the Made in Victoria strategy to strengthen local manufacturing capability and supply chain resilience. Grants support capital investment and business transformation.
grants.amount_note: Grants up to $250K with co-contribution of at least 50%. Supports capital equipment, tech adoption, and process improvement.

grants.name: QLD Business Growth Fund
grants.agency: Department of Employment, Small Business and Training (DESBT)
grants.description: Queensland state government program providing grants to high-growth small and medium businesses to accelerate growth. Supports businesses accessing new markets, adopting advanced technologies, creating jobs, and improving productivity. Requires co-investment and a clear growth strategy.
grants.amount_note: Grants up to $75K. Co-contribution of at least 25%–50% required. For market expansion, tech adoption, productivity.

grants.name: QLD Ignite Ideas Fund
grants.agency: Department of Environment, Tourism, Science and Innovation
grants.description: Queensland government grant supporting innovative businesses to commercialise new products and services that will create jobs and drive economic growth. Tiered funding: Tier 1 up to $100K for early-stage, Tier 2 up to $500K for growth-stage commercialisation. Strong focus on scaling Queensland innovation.
grants.amount_note: Tier 1: up to $100K. Tier 2: up to $500K. Matched co-investment required. For product commercialisation and market entry.

grants.name: Defence Industry Capability Grants
grants.agency: Department of Defence / Centre for Defence Industry Capability (CDIC)
grants.description: Federal grant program supporting Australian SMEs to build defence industry capability, win defence contracts, and integrate into global defence supply chains. Includes capability improvement grants, defence readiness assessments, export facilitation, and skilling support. Critical for Australia's sovereign defence industrial base.
grants.amount_note: Capability grants up to $250K. Includes matched funding for defence readiness, skilling, and export readiness. Multiple streams.

grants.name: ARENA Renewable Energy & Cleantech Grants
grants.description: ARENA provides grant funding for Australian businesses and researchers developing, demonstrating, and deploying renewable energy and clean technology solutions. Covers solar, hydrogen, energy storage, grid integration, biofuels, energy efficiency, and emerging low-emissions technologies. Supports projects from early-stage research through to commercial deployment.
grants.amount_note: Grants from $100K to $100M+. Typically 30%–50% of project costs. Larger for strategic national projects. Multiple funding rounds.

grants.name: Cyber Security Small Business Grants
grants.description: Federal program providing grants and rebates to help Australian small businesses improve their cybersecurity posture. Includes funding for cybersecurity assessments, security software, employee training, and implementing Essential Eight mitigation strategies. Supports Australia's cyber resilience.
grants.amount_note: Up to $15K for cybersecurity improvements, training, and software. Periodic rounds. Focus on practical cyber defence.
